name: Jejueo

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Jejueo (jeju1234).

notes: Koreanic language of Jeju Island, written in Hangul (including some conservative jamo such as the arae-a). Hangul is a featural alphabet, but its text is encoded as precomposed syllable blocks whose realization depends on syllable-final neutralization, resyllabification and morphophonemic sandhi across word boundaries. Recovering grapheme-to-IPA therefore requires a per-word pronunciation/lexicon stratum plus jamo decomposition that the engine does not bundle, so engine coverage here is lexicon-dependent and effectively zero without that stratum. Left empty deliberately rather than faking single-codepoint syllable rules; tone/pitch is not encoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Jejueo (jeju1234)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
